DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT JOHN JEW MOTION FOR ORDER IMPOSING TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE PRO PER PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO PROVIDE RESPONSES TO SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY AND HIS FAILURE TO PAY $360 IN SANCTIONS DOES NOT WARRANT TERMINATING SANCTIONS AT THIS TIME. HOWEVER, IF PLAINTIFF DOES NOT RESPOND TO THE SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER, HE RISKS TERMINATING SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE THIS ORDER TO PLAINTIFF ALONG WITH A COVER LETTER ADVISING HIM IN SIMPLE UNDERSTANDABLE LANGUAGE THAT PLAINTIFF RISKS LOSING THE CASE ALTOGETHER IF HE DOES NOT TIMELY RESPOND TO THE SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF MARTINEZ, JOSE SERVED JAN-04-2013, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON CORPORATION AS TO DEFENDANT JEW, JOHN C.
PLAINTIFF(S) REJECTED PROPOSED ARBITRATOR JOHN A. MCGUINN.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE LAW OFFICES OF VANN H. SLATTER'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF JOSE MARTINEZ - THE MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO CCP 1005 AND CCP 12C. THE MOVING PARTY FAILS TO PROVIDE A TIMELY PROOF OF SERVICE. THE MOVING PARTY MAIL-SERVED THE MOTION ON JULY 24, 2013, AND ONLY PROVIDED 15 COURT DAY NOTICE. CCP 1005 REQUIRES AT LEAST 16 COURT DAY NOTICE AND AN ADDITIONAL 5 CALENDAR DAYS FOR MAIL SERVICE. JUDGE: LESLIE NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/LN

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling for DEFENDANT JOHN JEW Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND DISMISSES WITH PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF JOSE MARTINEZ'S COMPLAINT IN ITS ENTIRETY. Judge Pro Tem: Mark Schreiber; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN JEW Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions is denied. The pro per Plaintiff's failure to provide responses to supplemental discovery and his failure to pay $360 in sanctions does not warrant terminating sanctions at this time. However, if Plaintiff does not respond to the supplemental discovery within 20 days of this Order, he risks terminating sanctions. Defendant shall serve this Order to Plaintiff along with a cover letter advising him in simple understandable language that Plaintiff risks losing the case altogether if he does not timely respond to the supplemental discovery. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Supplemental Interrogatory And Supplemental Demand For Inspection
Off Calendar Feb-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Martin Mertes, Esq. of Carbone Smoke Smith Bent & Leonard, Tel. 408.983.1675, for defendant and moving party, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court received courtesy copies of the moving papers and proof of service. DEFENDANT JOHN JEW's Motion To Compel Responses To Supplemental Interrogatory And Supplemental Demand For Inspection is granted. Request for sanctions is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $360 is awarded to defendant's counsel. The Court modified and signed the proposed order submitted by email. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bernard J. Maryanski, Esq. of the Law Office of Bernard J. Maryanski, Tel. 650.697.5524, appearing specially for Van H. Slatter, counsel for plaintiff, and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. For good cause, the Court granted COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF JOSE MARTINEZ' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL AS ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Trial date is not set. Counsel must provide a proposed Order on the required Judicial Council form. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Aug-14-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHILIP T. KILDUFF, TEL. NO. 673-0368, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE MOVING PARTY;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. THE LAW OFFICES OF VANN H. SLATTER'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF JOSE MARTINEZ - The motion is off calendar pursuant to CCP 1005 and CCP 12c. The moving party fails to provide a timely proof of service. The moving party mail-served the motion on July 24, 2013, and only provided 15 court day notice. CCP 1005 requires at least 16 court day notice and an additional 5 calendar days for mail service. Judge: Leslie Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/LN
